hypothesis: If orthogonal, property-specific embeddings learned by a concept bottleneck model are added as steering vectors to a frozen LLM’s intermediate activations during generation, then the model will more accurately hit specified molecular property targets (lower MAE/Best100MAE) across in- and out-of-distribution settings while preserving validity, uniqueness, novelty, and synthetic accessibility and outperforming vanilla, ActAdd, and fine-tuning baselines because these embeddings disentangle and align controllable property directions in the model’s representation space.
